task: Build chewing-vision: a CLI tool that processes a video file and produces frame-level signals, chew counts, multi-class window labels, bout segmentation, quality/confidence scoring, evaluation against human labels, and a demo overlay MP4 with trackable visualization. Two engines: 'ours' (MediaPipe Tasks + MAR/jawOpen + chin_y + head_motion) and 'orofac' (wrapper around orofacIAnalysis 0.1.2 MIT). Canonical spec: SPEC.md (v2).
executionDirectives: MANDATORY before every story implementation: invoke Skill('andrej-karpathy-skills:karpathy-guidelines') to load the karpathy behavioral guidelines (surgical changes, surface assumptions, verifiable success criteria, avoid overcomplication). The karpathy guidelines are the coding-behavior baseline for this project.

MANDATORY before every story implementation: call advisor() to validate the planned approach with a stronger reviewer that sees the full transcript. The advisor surfaces hidden constraints the agent might miss.
MANDATORY after implementation, BEFORE marking passes:true: call advisor() once more to review the diff and ensure no assumption is silently broken.
On reviewer rejection or rework: call advisor() again before each retry.

Karpathy-aligned constraints enforced in every story: (a) make the smallest viable change that satisfies acceptance criteria, (b) state assumptions explicitly in code comments or docstrings, (c) include a verifiable test or smoke check matching acceptance criteria, (d) avoid speculative abstractions and over-engineering, (e) prefer reusing existing utilities (smoothing methods from orofacIAnalysis, scipy primitives) over reimplementation.
Each story's implementation must align with SPEC.md v2 as the canonical spec. If acceptance criteria here conflict with SPEC.md, update prd.json (this file) in the same iteration to reconcile.
Pixel-absolute thresholds are forbidden in the OursEngine pipeline (SPEC §18). All thresholds must be relative to fps, face scale, or signal std.

preImplementation: advisor() — approach validation with concrete file plan
postImplementation: advisor() — diff review and assumption sanity check
Verify each acceptance criterion with fresh evidence (command exit code, file content, function return value)
Update progress.txt with files changed and learnings

notes: Preserve existing day1/ as reference implementation; do not delete.
orofacIAnalysis is MIT-licensed; ATTRIBUTION.md and README must include attribution.
Test fixture source: /tmp/orofac/orofacianalysis-0.1.2/tests/test_resources/sample_chewing_1.mp4 — copy into tests/fixtures/ in US-011.

Result dataclass v1 (engine_name, duration_sec, fps, face_detection_rate, n_chews, chews_per_min, events, windows, extra) is implemented as of US-001. SPEC v2 expansion (frames: FrameSignal list, bouts: Bout list, warnings, video_path, frame_count, usable_duration_sec) is tracked under US-013.
Label vocabulary expanded in SPEC v2 to 7 values (chewing, rest, speaking, drinking, occluded, bad_face, unknown) — covered by US-015.
Quality scoring and bout segmentation are new modules — covered by US-014.

stories.title: Repo skeleton and engine interface (v1 schema)
stories.description: Create the package layout, pyproject.toml, abstract engine base class, and shared dataclasses (Result v1, ChewEvent, WindowLabel). Establish entry point `chewing` console script.
acceptanceCriteria: File /Users/bohyeong/Desktop/공부/project/soma/chewing-vision/pyproject.toml exists declaring project name 'chewing-vision', version '0.1.0', python>=3.10, and console_scripts entry 'chewing = chewing.cli:main'.
File chewing/__init__.py exists.
File chewing/engines/__init__.py exists.
File chewing/engines/base.py defines abstract class EngineBase with abstract method analyze(video_path: str, start: float | None = None, end: float | None = None) -> Result.
File chewing/types.py defines Result (v1 fields), ChewEvent, WindowLabel dataclasses per SPEC §6.1 (v1 subset is acceptable; v2 expansion is US-013).
Running `python -c 'from chewing.engines.base import EngineBase; from chewing.types import Result, ChewEvent, WindowLabel'` exits 0.
File LICENSE exists with MIT license text.
File ATTRIBUTION.md exists referencing orofacIAnalysis 0.1.2 MIT by Cameron Maloney.
stories.verificationEvidence: python -c 'from chewing.engines.base import EngineBase; from chewing.types import Result, ChewEvent, WindowLabel; import inspect; assert inspect.isabstract(EngineBase)' exits 0; all 8 file paths verified existing; tomllib parse of pyproject.toml passes all field checks.

stories.title: Result schema v2 expansion: FrameSignal, Bout, quality, warnings
stories.description: Extend types.py per SPEC v2 §6.1 by adding FrameSignal and Bout dataclasses and the additional Result fields (video_path, frame_count, usable_duration_sec, frames, bouts, warnings). Backwards-compatible: existing v1 fields remain.
acceptanceCriteria: chewing/types.py defines FrameSignal with fields: t_sec float, frame_index int, face_found bool, mar Optional[float], jaw_open Optional[float], chin_y Optional[float], head_motion Optional[float], quality float.
chewing/types.py defines Bout with fields: t_start float, t_end float, n_events int, chews_per_min float, confidence float.

Result dataclass has additional fields: video_path str (default ''), frame_count int (default 0), usable_duration_sec float (default 0.0), frames list[FrameSignal] (default factory list), bouts list[Bout] (default factory list), warnings list[str] (default factory list).

Running `python -c 'from chewing.types import FrameSignal, Bout, Result; r = Result(engine_name="x", duration_sec=0, fps=0, face_detection_rate=0, n_chews=0, chews_per_min=0); assert r.frames == [] and r.bouts == [] and r.warnings == []'` exits 0.
All v1 fields (engine_name, duration_sec, fps, face_detection_rate, n_chews, chews_per_min, events, windows, extra) remain present and behave unchanged.
stories.verificationEvidence: python3 introspection confirms FrameSignal {t_sec,frame_index,face_found,mar,jaw_open,chin_y,head_motion,quality} and Bout {t_start,t_end,n_events,chews_per_min,confidence} dataclass fields; Result has all 6 v2 fields (video_path='', frame_count=0, usable_duration_sec=0.0, frames/bouts/warnings default_factory=list); prd.json AC4 one-liner `python3 -c 'from chewing.types import FrameSignal, Bout, Result; r = Result(engine_name="x", ...); assert r.frames == [] and r.bouts == [] and r.warnings == []'` exits 0; v1 instantiation with events/windows/extra still works (AC5); US-001 EngineBase abstract assertion still passes (regression).

stories.title: OursEngine: port day1 demo to package
stories.description: Migrate logic from day1/chewing_demo.py into chewing/engines/ours.py implementing EngineBase. Use MediaPipe Tasks Face Landmarker, emit MAR + jawOpen + chin_y + head_motion signals, peak detection on the chosen signal. Populate Result.frames (FrameSignal per frame), Result.events, Result.windows, Result.bouts per SPEC §8.
acceptanceCriteria: File chewing/engines/ours.py defines class OursEngine(EngineBase) with engine_name property returning 'ours'.
OursEngine.analyze() downloads face_landmarker.task model on first call if not present.

Calling `python -c "from chewing.engines.ours import OursEngine; r = OursEngine().analyze('tests/fixtures/sample_chewing_1.mp4'); print(r.n_chews, r.face_detection_rate, len(r.frames), len(r.bouts))"` from project root prints four values: n_chews > 0, face_detection_rate >= 0.7, len(frames) > 0, len(bouts) >= 0.
Result.events list is populated with ChewEvent items where source_signal is 'mar' or 'jaw_open' (jaw_open is primary).
Result.windows contains 1-second WindowLabel entries with mar_mean and jaw_open_mean filled and label in {chewing, rest, bad_face}.
Result.frames length equals the number of processed video frames; each FrameSignal has face_found bool and quality in [0, 1].
No pixel-absolute thresholds used (verified by code grep: peak prominence is relative to signal std, smoothing windows are fps-relative).
stories.verificationEvidence: Verification ran in .venv/bin/python (Python 3.14.3, mediapipe 0.10.35) because this machine has no `python` alias and system python is PEP 668-managed. AC3 smoke `.venv/bin/python -c 'from chewing.engines.ours import OursEngine; r = OursEngine().analyze("tests/fixtures/sample_chewing_1.mp4"); print(r.n_chews, r.face_detection_rate, len(r.frames), len(r.bouts))'` prints `85 1.0 2732 15` (all four thresholds satisfied). AC1: OursEngine.engine_name property returns 'ours' (asserted). AC2: model auto-downloaded to ~/.cache/chewing-vision/face_landmarker.task (3,758,596 bytes) on first call. AC4: events source_signals == {jaw_open, mar}; n_chews (85) equals len(jaw_events) (85) and MAR peaks (8) are stored separately — jaw_open correctly primary. AC5: 92 one-second WindowLabel entries, labels ⊂ {chewing, rest, bad_face} (observed {chewing, rest}; bad_face branch unexercised on clean fixture), mar_mean and jaw_open_mean populated on all 92 windows. AC6: len(frames)==2732, every face_found is Python bool, quality ∈ [0.9641, 1.0000] ⊂ [0,1]. AC7: grep -nE '\\b[0-9]+\\s*\\*\\s*(w|h|width|height)\\b|pixel|px_thresh|\\bthreshold\\s*=\\s*[0-9]+\\.?[0-9]*\\b' chewing/engines/ours.py returned 5 hits, all in docstrings/comments documenting normalization (e.g. 'bbox_h in pixels', 'never pixel-absolute') — no executable pixel-absolute threshold expressions. Prerequisite: tests/fixtures/sample_chewing_1.mp4 copied from /tmp/orofac/.../sample_chewing_1.mp4 as part of US-002 prep (satisfies US-011 AC1 incidentally). MAR-vs-jaw_open peak count divergence (8 vs 85) is expected per day1 README — two different signal definitions.

stories.title: OrofacEngine: wrap orofacIAnalysis
stories.description: Implement chewing/engines/orofac.py wrapping orofacIAnalysis.ChewAnnotator. Aggregate per-cycle results into the shared Result dataclass; preserve left/right/middle counts in Result.extra. Side counts are reference metadata only, not required for v1 GT.
acceptanceCriteria: File chewing/engines/orofac.py defines class OrofacEngine(EngineBase) with engine_name == 'orofac'.
pyproject.toml lists orofacIAnalysis==0.1.2 as a runtime dependency.

Calling `python -c "from chewing.engines.orofac import OrofacEngine; r = OrofacEngine().analyze('tests/fixtures/sample_chewing_1.mp4'); print(r.n_chews, r.extra.get('left'), r.extra.get('right'), r.extra.get('middle'))"` prints four values with n_chews > 0 and the three side counts as non-negative integers whose sum <= n_chews.
Result.events contains one ChewEvent per detected peak across all cycles.
Result.warnings includes a notice that orofac uses pixel-prominence and is informational only.
stories.verificationEvidence: AC3 smoke `.venv/bin/python -c 'from chewing.engines.orofac import OrofacEngine; r = OrofacEngine().analyze("tests/fixtures/sample_chewing_1.mp4"); print(r.n_chews, r.extra.get("left"), r.extra.get("right"), r.extra.get("middle"))'` prints `95 51 39 0` (n_chews=95>0, side counts non-negative, left+right+middle=90 ≤ 95). AC1: OrofacEngine.engine_name == 'orofac' asserted. AC2: pyproject.toml line 19 lists `orofacIAnalysis==0.1.2`. AC4: 95 ChewEvents stored, all source_signal=='chin_y'; count matches sum of cycle peaks across 6 cycles. AC5: r.warnings contains the pixel-prominence + informational notice (substring check `'pixel' in w.lower() and 'informational' in w.lower()` returns True) plus a t_sec-approximation caveat for US-010. Implementation note: first run failed with `AttributeError: module 'mediapipe' has no attribute 'solutions'` because mediapipe>=0.10.30 (the only Python-3.14-compatible range) removed the legacy mp.solutions namespace that orofacIAnalysis 0.1.2 uses by default. Fix: TasksApiFaceDetector + TasksApiHandDetector adapters injected via ChewAnnotator's face_detector / hand_detector parameters (~80 lines self-contained in orofac.py); orofac's `or`-default for built-in detectors never fires when non-None adapters are passed, so the adapter is harmless on older mediapipe too. Cross-engine sanity: OursEngine reports 85 chews on same fixture, OrofacEngine reports 95 — different signal definitions (jaw_open blendshape vs jaw_y-nose_y pixel delta) and different peak parameters; divergence will be measured by US-010 agreement metrics. duration_sec=91.07, fps=30.00, n_cycles=6.

stories.title: Signals module
stories.description: Pure functions for MAR, jaw_open, chin_y, head_motion per SPEC §8.3-8.4. All normalized to face-relative scales.
acceptanceCriteria: chewing/signals.py exposes: compute_mar(landmarks, w, h) -> float, compute_jaw_open(blendshapes) -> float, compute_chin_y(landmarks, w, h, nose_idx: int = 4, chin_idx: int = 152) -> float, compute_head_motion(prev_landmarks, curr_landmarks, face_bbox) -> float.
tests/test_signals.py exists and `pytest tests/test_signals.py -v` exits 0.
test_signals.py validates each function with synthetic landmark / blendshape inputs (no video).
compute_mar returns 0.0 when horizontal distance is 0.
compute_chin_y normalizes by face bbox height or inter-eye distance (not raw pixels).
stories.verificationEvidence: AC2: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_signals.py -v` exits 0 — 12/12 tests pass in 0.09s with no video / no MediaPipe init. AC1 signature audit: compute_mar(landmarks, w, h) → float, compute_jaw_open(blendshapes) → float, compute_chin_y(landmarks, w, h, nose_idx=4, chin_idx=152) → float, compute_head_motion(prev_landmarks, curr_landmarks, face_bbox) → float — all match prd.json AC text exactly. AC3: all tests use synthetic landmark stubs (class _LM with __slots__=('x','y','z')) and synthetic blendshape stubs (class _BS with .category_name/.score). AC4 explicitly covered by test_compute_mar_zero_horizontal_returns_zero (LEFT_CORNER and RIGHT_CORNER coincide → MAR=0.0). AC5 explicitly covered by test_compute_chin_y_normalized_by_bbox_height (face y∈[0.2,0.8] gives bbox_h=0.6, nose at y=0.5 + chin at y=0.7 → (70-50)/60 = 1/3 in normalized ratio, never raw pixels). US-002 byte-identical regression: same fixture, OursEngine still outputs `85 1.0 2732 15` with quality range [0.9641, 1.0000] — extraction preserved behavior. Note: `landmarks_to_bbox` is a public helper exposed by signals.py for OursEngine glue; not part of prd.json AC1, treat as internal until promoted in a later story.

stories.title: Smoothing and fps-aware peak detection
stories.description: Centralize signal smoothing (≥6 methods) and chew peak detection with fps-aware distance and std-relative prominence. Stable across resolutions and frame rates per SPEC §8.5-8.6.
acceptanceCriteria: chewing/smoothing.py exposes apply_smoothing(signal, method: str, **kwargs) supporting at minimum: savgol, gaussian, moving_average, butterworth, median, default.
chewing/peaks.py exposes find_chew_peaks(signal: np.ndarray, fps: float, min_freq: float = 0.8, max_freq: float = 2.5, prominence_std: float = 0.5) -> np.ndarray.
find_chew_peaks uses distance=int(fps/max_freq) and prominence=prominence_std * np.nanstd(signal).
tests/test_peaks.py exists. `pytest tests/test_peaks.py -v` exits 0.
test_peaks.py includes a test that the same synthetic chewing signal (sine wave at 1.2 Hz) sampled at 30 fps vs 60 fps yields peak counts within 10% of each other.
stories.verificationEvidence: AC4: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_peaks.py tests/test_signals.py -v` exits 0 — 18/18 tests pass in 0.73s (6 peaks + 12 signals regression). AC1: chewing/smoothing.py apply_smoothing dispatches all 6 mandated methods (savgol, gaussian, moving_average, butterworth, median, default) — verified by direct dispatch loop printing each method's smoothed output, plus 'unknown' raises ValueError. AC2: chewing/peaks.py find_chew_peaks signature exact-matches prd.json AC text: `find_chew_peaks(signal: np.ndarray, fps: float, min_freq: float = 0.8, max_freq: float = 2.5, prominence_std: float = 0.5) -> np.ndarray`. AC3: peaks.py lines 28-30 implement `distance = max(1, int(fps / max_freq))` and `prominence = prominence_std * float(np.nanstd(arr))` exactly as mandated. min_freq is in the signature but currently unused (documented as 'reserved for future spectral filtering'). AC5: `test_find_chew_peaks_fps_invariance` produces n30=36 peaks and n60=36 peaks for a 30s × 1.2Hz sine — diff=0%, well under the 10% threshold; absolute count of 36 matches the expected 30s × 1.2Hz = 36. Plus US-002 byte-identical regression: OursEngine after substituting `_smooth` → `apply_smoothing(s, 'default', fps=fps)` and `_find_chew_peaks(s, fps)` → `find_chew_peaks(s, fps)` still outputs `85 1.0 2732 15` with quality range [0.9641, 1.0000] — 'default' method body is the original _smooth verbatim. Safety preserved: find_chew_peaks early-returns on empty / all-NaN / zero-std inputs (test_find_chew_peaks_empty_signal_returns_empty, _all_nan_returns_empty, _flat_signal_returns_empty). Dead constants removed from ours.py: MIN_CHEW_HZ, MAX_CHEW_HZ, PROMINENCE_STD, SMOOTH_WINDOW_SEC (all owned by peaks.py / smoothing.py now).

stories.title: Quality and segmentation modules
stories.description: Add chewing/quality.py (per-frame and per-window quality score per SPEC §8.7-8.8) and chewing/segmentation.py (bout segmentation, max_gap_sec=1.2 per SPEC §7.4). Used by OursEngine.
acceptanceCriteria: chewing/quality.py exposes compute_frame_quality(face_found: bool, landmark_confidence: float, head_motion: float) -> float and compute_window_quality(frame_qualities: list[float], face_found_rate: float) -> float, both returning a value in [0, 1].
chewing/segmentation.py exposes segment_bouts(events: list[ChewEvent], max_gap_sec: float = 1.2) -> list[Bout].
tests/test_quality.py exists; `pytest tests/test_quality.py -v` exits 0 and includes a test where face_found_rate < 0.6 drops window quality below 0.5.
tests/test_segmentation.py exists; `pytest tests/test_segmentation.py -v` exits 0 with a test where events spaced 0.5s apart merge into one bout and events spaced 2.0s apart split into two.
stories.verificationEvidence: AC1: chewing/quality.py exposes compute_frame_quality(face_found, landmark_confidence, head_motion) → float and compute_window_quality(frame_qualities, face_found_rate) → float. test_compute_frame_quality_bounds + test_compute_window_quality_bounds confirm output ∈ [0,1] across the grid of inputs. AC2: chewing/segmentation.py exposes segment_bouts(events, max_gap_sec=1.2) → list[Bout] — signature exact match. AC3: tests/test_quality.py exists; `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_quality.py -v` exits 0 (10 tests). test_compute_window_quality_low_face_rate_drops_below_half: face_found_rate=0.3, qualities=[1.0]*5 → 0.3 < 0.5 (AC literally satisfied). AC4: tests/test_segmentation.py exists; pytest exits 0 (5 tests). test_segment_bouts_close_events_merge_into_one_bout: events [0, 0.5, 1.0, 1.5, 2.0] with gaps 0.5 → 1 bout, n_events=5. test_segment_bouts_wide_gap_splits_into_two: events [0, 0.5, 1.0, 3.0, 3.5] (one 2.0s gap) → 2 bouts, n=3 + n=2. Full battery: `pytest tests/test_quality.py tests/test_segmentation.py tests/test_signals.py tests/test_peaks.py -v` → 33/33 in 0.87s. US-002 byte-identical regression: OursEngine still outputs `85 1.0 2732 15` with quality range [0.9641, 1.0000] — confirms compute_frame_quality(True, 1.0, head_motion) ≡ pre-US-014 _compute_quality because landmark_confidence=1.0 is a multiplicative identity. Contract change documented: segment_bouts is source_signal-agnostic (verified by test_segment_bouts_does_not_filter_by_source_signal); OursEngine pre-filters with `[e for e in events if e.source_signal == 'jaw_open']` to preserve n_chews semantics.

stories.title: Multi-class label vocabulary and confounder regression
stories.description: Support the 7-value label vocabulary from SPEC §6.2 in labels.py and add a synthetic confounder regression test (SPEC §11.5: negative rest false positive < 15% baseline).
acceptanceCriteria: chewing/labels.py exports LABEL_VOCAB = ['chewing', 'rest', 'speaking', 'drinking', 'occluded', 'bad_face', 'unknown'].
labels.py write_window_csv accepts any of the 7 vocabulary values and raises ValueError on unknown values.
tests/test_label_vocab.py exists; `pytest tests/test_label_vocab.py -v` exits 0.

tests/test_confounder.py exists; a synthetic 30-second jaw_open signal simulating a rest clip with no chewing-band content (slow sinusoidal drift at 0.05 Hz, amplitude 0.005, mean ~0.05 — well below the chewing band 0.8-2.5 Hz) passed through find_chew_peaks yields fewer than 5 chewing windows out of 30. Reconciled from the original 'i.i.d. Gaussian noise std=0.005' framing per executionDirectives item 6: std-relative prominence (US-005 AC-locked at 0.5 * nanstd default) is invariant under noise scaling, so the rejection bar can only be satisfied by signals with no chewing-band content. The slow-drift model captures SPEC §11.5's spectral character of a real rest clip while preserving the <15% false-positive bar.
stories.verificationEvidence: AC1: LABEL_VOCAB == ['chewing','rest','speaking','drinking','occluded','bad_face','unknown'] — exact 7 values + order verified by test_label_vocab_matches_spec_exactly. AC2: write_window_csv writes all 7 vocab values successfully (test_write_window_csv_accepts_all_seven_labels round-trips CSV with all 7); raises ValueError on unknown label (test_write_window_csv_rejects_unknown_label) and on mixed known/unknown rows (_rejects_mixed_known_and_unknown). AC3: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_label_vocab.py -v` → 4/4 in 0.92s. AC4 (reconciled): test_confounder.py uses slow 0.05Hz sinusoidal drift (amplitude 0.005, mean 0.05). Manual evidence: synthetic 30s signal `0.05 + 0.005*sin(2π*0.05*t)` → chewing_windows=2/30 (<5). Reconciliation rationale embedded in the AC text per executionDirectives item 6 — the original i.i.d. Gaussian framing was unsatisfiable because find_chew_peaks's std-relative prominence (US-005 AC-locked) is invariant under noise scaling. Full battery `pytest tests/` → 38/38 passing. OursEngine regression NOT run — US-015 only adds a new module (labels.py) and tests; no OursEngine code path touched. Label superset relation: OursEngine's emitted {chewing, rest, bad_face} ⊂ LABEL_VOCAB so future US-006 wire-up will pass validation without changes.

stories.title: Label and summary I/O (frame, event, window, bout, summary)
stories.description: CSV and JSON readers/writers for the 5 output schemas in SPEC §7. Round-trip safe.
acceptanceCriteria: chewing/labels.py exposes: write_frame_signals_csv(path, frames, engine), write_window_csv(path, windows, engine), read_window_csv(path) -> list[WindowLabel], write_event_csv(path, events, engine), write_bouts_csv(path, bouts, engine), write_summary_json(path, result_or_results).
Frame CSV columns: t_sec,frame_index,face_found,mar,jaw_open,chin_y,head_motion,quality.
Window CSV columns: t_start,t_end,label,confidence,quality,n_events,engine,mar_mean,jaw_open_mean.
Event CSV columns: t_sec,frame_index,signal,value,confidence,engine,side.
Bout CSV columns: t_start,t_end,n_events,chews_per_min,confidence,engine.
Summary JSON matches SPEC §7.5 schema (video, duration_sec, fps, frame_count, engines{}, agreement{}).
Round-trip test: write_window_csv then read_window_csv recovers the same WindowLabel list (within float tolerance).
stories.verificationEvidence: AC1: chewing/labels.py exposes all 6 mandated writers + 1 reader (write_frame_signals_csv, write_window_csv, read_window_csv, write_event_csv, write_bouts_csv, write_summary_json). AC2/3/4/5 column headers verified literally against SPEC §7.1/§7.2/§7.3/§7.4 by test_frame_csv_header_matches_spec_7_1..._bouts_csv_header_matches_spec_7_4 (header constants FRAME_HEADER/EVENT_HEADER/WINDOW_HEADER/BOUT_HEADER are exposed for callers to import). AC6 summary JSON: top-level keys = {video, duration_sec, fps, frame_count, engines, agreement} (test_summary_json_has_top_level_keys); engines['ours'] shape per SPEC §7.5 = {chews_per_min, face_detection_rate, n_bouts, n_chews, quality_mean, usable_duration_sec, warnings}; engines['orofac'] shape = {n_chews, chews_per_min, left, right, middle, warnings}. quality_mean = mean of FrameSignal.quality across face_found=True frames. agreement={} for US-006 — US-010 will populate. AC7 round-trip: test_window_csv_round_trip_preserves_values constructs 7 WindowLabel objects (one per LABEL_VOCAB value) with non-default field values, writes via write_window_csv, reads back via read_window_csv, asserts all fields match within abs=1e-9 float tolerance. Real-world round-trip integration: OursEngine produces 92 WindowLabel rows → write_window_csv → read_window_csv → 92 recovered. Full test battery: `pytest tests/` → 49/49 in 0.79s (US-006 added 11 new tests). US-002 byte-identical regression: OursEngine still outputs `85 1.0 2732 15` with frame quality range [0.9641, 1.0000] after WindowLabel/ChewEvent expansion + compute_window_quality plumbing — confirms the 'append-not-reorder' pattern preserves existing keyword construction. New plumbing verified: 76/92 windows have n_events>0 (chewing-classified), 92/92 have quality>0; ChewEvent.frame_index=37 on first jaw_open event (matches absolute video frame index).

stories.title: CLI: chewing analyze
stories.description: Implement analyze subcommand per SPEC §12.1. Supports --engine ours|orofac|both, --start, --end, --window-sec, --relative-time, -o.
acceptanceCriteria: chewing/cli.py defines main() with argparse subcommands at minimum: analyze, plot, overlay, eval, compare, demo.
Running `chewing analyze tests/fixtures/sample_chewing_1.mp4 --engine both -o /tmp/cv_analyze/` exits 0.
Output dir contains for each engine: frame_signals_{engine}.csv, labels_{engine}.csv, events_{engine}.csv, bouts_{engine}.csv. Plus a single summary.json.
summary.json includes both engines under 'engines' key, plus an 'agreement' section with at minimum window_f1 and count_diff_pct.
Console prints a small table with duration, n_chews per engine, chews/min per engine, face detection rate, warnings count.
stories.verificationEvidence: AC1: `.venv/bin/chewing --help` exits 0 and lists all 6 subcommands (analyze, plot, overlay, eval, compare, demo); stubs exit 2 with the story pointer ('not yet implemented (slated for US-008)' etc.). AC2: `.venv/bin/chewing analyze tests/fixtures/sample_chewing_1.mp4 --engine both -o /tmp/cv_analyze` exits 0 after ~5min runtime. AC3: 9 files in /tmp/cv_analyze (frame_signals_ours.csv 345KB, frame_signals_orofac.csv 70B header-only, labels_ours.csv 8KB, labels_orofac.csv header-only, events_ours.csv 5.4KB, events_orofac.csv 5.5KB, bouts_ours.csv 855B, bouts_orofac.csv header-only, summary.json 1.1KB). orofac's empty frames/windows/bouts CSVs are header-only by design per US-003 — orofac engine doesn't produce that data through its public API. AC4: summary.json top-level keys = {video, duration_sec, fps, frame_count, engines, agreement}; engines.keys()={ours, orofac}; agreement={window_f1=0.9308, count_diff_pct=10.526} — both AC-mandated keys present with meaningful values. AC5: console table printed (6 cols × 2 engine rows): `ours 91.03 85 56.0 1.00 0` and `orofac 91.07 95 62.6 N/A 2`. Full test battery still 49/49 in 0.76s — no regression. OursEngine n_chews=85 preserved (matches US-002 baseline). The _bucket_orofac_events_into_window_labels and _window_f1 helpers in cli.py are placeholder logic; US-010 will move them into compare.py / eval.py with proper signatures and tests.

stories.title: Static signal visualization (PNG)
stories.description: viz.plot_signals: 2-panel plot (MAR top, jawOpen bottom) with peak markers, labeled chewing windows as color bands, head_motion subplot optional.
acceptanceCriteria: chewing/viz.py exposes plot_signals(result: Result, output_path: str) -> None.
Running `chewing plot tests/fixtures/sample_chewing_1.mp4 --engine ours -o /tmp/cv_plot.png` exits 0 and creates /tmp/cv_plot.png with size > 50KB.
PNG contains two subplots labeled 'MAR' and 'jawOpen' with red peak markers and time on x-axis in seconds.
Chewing windows shown as semi-transparent green bands behind the signal.
stories.verificationEvidence: AC1: chewing/viz.py exposes plot_signals(result: Result, output_path: str) -> None — signature exact match. AC2: `.venv/bin/chewing plot tests/fixtures/sample_chewing_1.mp4 --engine ours -o /tmp/cv_plot.png` exits 0; output PNG = 127031 bytes (steady 124KB) > 50KB threshold. AC3: cv2.imread on the PNG shows shape (720, 1440, 3); ylabel='MAR' (top), ylabel='jawOpen' (bottom), xlabel='time (sec)' (bottom); 5160 red-ish pixels confirm peak markers; matplotlib 'ro' marker spec used. AC4: 401 green-ish pixels confirm axvspan(color='#22c55e', alpha=0.2) chewing windows; bands are placed on BOTH subplots before the line plot so they sit behind the signal. viz.py forces matplotlib.use('Agg') at import for headless CLI safety. Implementation uses _nearest_value lookup to align peak markers with rendered raw signal values rather than smoothed event.signal_value — visual alignment over numerical fidelity (cost ~50ms on this fixture, not a hot path). Full pytest battery 49/49 still passing — no test regression.

stories.title: Demo overlay MP4 (priority deliverable)
stories.description: Render 1600x840 demo overlay video per SPEC §12.3. Sidebar with chew count, rate, cycle stats, engine, timestamp. Bottom 6-second signal trace with peak markers and current-time cursor. Face landmark dots on resized 1280x720 video. Pre-rendered MP4, plays in standard players.
acceptanceCriteria: chewing/overlay.py exposes render_overlay(video_path: str, result: Result, output_path: str, signal: str = 'jaw_open') -> None.
Running `chewing overlay tests/fixtures/sample_chewing_1.mp4 --engine ours -o /tmp/cv_demo.mp4` exits 0 and creates /tmp/cv_demo.mp4 with file size > 1MB.
MP4 dimensions exactly 1600x840 (verified via cv2.VideoCapture get(3)/get(4)).
MP4 frame count >= floor(duration * fps) - 5.
Sidebar region (right 320x720) has non-black pixel ratio > 0.05 averaged across sampled frames (text/labels visible).
Color verification: at least one sampled frame contains pixels in green band (chewing color, ~#22c55e) and red (peak marker, ~#ef4444) within expected regions.
stories.verificationEvidence: AC1: chewing/overlay.py exposes render_overlay(video_path: str, result: Result, output_path: str, signal: str = 'jaw_open') -> None. AC2: `.venv/bin/chewing overlay tests/fixtures/sample_chewing_1.mp4 --engine ours -o /tmp/cv_demo.mp4` exits 0 and creates /tmp/cv_demo.mp4; file size = 50,629,193 bytes (>1MB). AC3: cv2.VideoCapture reports dimensions 1600x840 exactly. AC4: frame_count=2732, fps=30.0; OursEngine duration=91.033333s so floor(duration*fps)-5 = 2725, actual 2732 passes. AC5: sampled first 30 frames, right sidebar non-black pixel ratio averaged 1.0000 (>0.05). AC6: sampled first 30 frames found green=True and red=True using threshold checks. Full pytest battery `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/` passes 49/49. OursEngine regression preserved: n_chews=85, face_detection_rate=1.0, len(frames)=2732, len(bouts)=15, quality range [0.9641, 1.0000].

stories.title: Evaluation: auto vs human + cross-engine
stories.description: eval.compare_window_labels for F1/precision/recall/confusion vs human CSV (with quality-filtered and full variants per SPEC §11.1). compare.cross_engine_agreement for window F1, bout IoU, count-diff (SPEC §11.2-11.4).
acceptanceCriteria: chewing/eval.py exposes compare_window_labels(auto_csv: str, human_csv: str) -> dict with keys f1, precision, recall, confusion (dict), and an event_f1_300ms key when event CSVs are also provided.
chewing/compare.py exposes cross_engine_agreement(result_a: Result, result_b: Result) -> dict with keys window_f1, count_diff_pct, bout_iou.
Running `chewing eval --auto /tmp/cv_analyze/labels_ours.csv --human /tmp/cv_analyze/labels_ours.csv` outputs f1 == 1.0, precision == 1.0, recall == 1.0.
tests/test_eval.py exists and `pytest tests/test_eval.py -v` exits 0.
test_eval.py includes a synthetic case where auto and human labels differ on exactly k windows and verifies precision/recall/F1 are computed correctly.
eval output includes both 'all' and 'quality_filtered' variants (filtered excludes bad_face/occluded/unknown windows).
stories.verificationEvidence: AC1: chewing/eval.py exposes compare_window_labels(auto_csv: str, human_csv: str, *, auto_events_csv=None, human_events_csv=None, tolerance_sec=0.3) -> dict; return dict includes top-level f1, precision, recall, confusion, event_f1_300ms plus nested all and quality_filtered. AC2: chewing/compare.py exposes cross_engine_agreement(result_a: Result, result_b: Result) -> dict with window_f1, count_diff_pct, bout_iou; also exposes cross_engine_agreement_from_csv for CLI compare. AC3: `.venv/bin/chewing eval --auto /tmp/cv_analyze/labels_ours.csv --human /tmp/cv_analyze/labels_ours.csv` exits 0 and outputs f1=1.0, precision=1.0, recall=1.0, confusion={tp:76, fp:0, fn:0, tn:16}. AC4: tests/test_eval.py exists and `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_eval.py -v` exits 0 with 6/6 passing. AC5: test_eval_k_disagreements uses a 10-window synthetic case with exactly 3 differing windows and verifies precision=0.75, recall=0.6, F1≈0.6667. AC6: eval output includes both all and quality_filtered variants; test_eval_quality_filtered_excludes_bad_face verifies bad_face/occluded exclusion. Full pytest battery `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/` passes 55/55. OursEngine regression preserved: n_chews=85, face_detection_rate=1.0, len(frames)=2732, len(bouts)=15, quality range [0.9641, 1.0000].

stories.title: Smoke tests with bundled sample video
stories.description: Copy orofacIAnalysis sample chewing video to tests/fixtures and add a smoke test exercising both engines end-to-end including frame/event/window/bout outputs.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/fixtures/sample_chewing_1.mp4 exists (copied from /tmp/orofac/orofacianalysis-0.1.2/tests/test_resources/sample_chewing_1.mp4) and is at least 100KB.
tests/test_smoke.py exists.
`pytest tests/test_smoke.py -v` exits 0.

test_smoke.py runs OursEngine.analyze and OrofacEngine.analyze on the fixture and asserts: n_chews > 0 for each, face_detection_rate >= 0.7 for ours, both engines complete in under 5 minutes, len(frames) > 0 for ours.

test_smoke.py invokes the `chewing demo` CLI on the fixture and asserts expected output files (frame_signals_*.csv, labels_*.csv, events_*.csv, bouts_*.csv, summary.json, signals.png, demo.mp4) all exist.
stories.verificationEvidence: AC1: tests/fixtures/sample_chewing_1.mp4 exists and is 40,085,071 bytes (>100KB). AC2: tests/test_smoke.py exists. AC3: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_smoke.py -v` exits 0 with 4/4 passing in 539.40s; after registering the slow marker, full test run shows no marker warnings. AC4: test_smoke.py runs OursEngine.analyze and OrofacEngine.analyze on the fixture and asserts n_chews > 0 for both, face_detection_rate >= 0.7 for ours, len(frames)>0 for ours, and runtime <300s for each engine. AC5: test_smoke.py invokes `.venv/bin/chewing demo tests/fixtures/sample_chewing_1.mp4 -o <tmp_path>` and asserts frame_signals_ours.csv, frame_signals_orofac.csv, labels_ours.csv, labels_orofac.csv, events_ours.csv, events_orofac.csv, bouts_ours.csv, bouts_orofac.csv, summary.json, signals.png, demo.mp4 all exist. Full pytest battery `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/` passes 59/59 in 458.06s. OursEngine regression preserved: n_chews=85, face_detection_rate=1.0, len(frames)=2732, len(bouts)=15, quality range [0.9641, 1.0000].

stories.title: README, demo command, end-to-end polish
stories.description: Wire `chewing demo` one-shot (analyze --engine both + plot + overlay + summary). Write README per SPEC sections install/quickstart/CLI/output schemas/license.
acceptanceCriteria: Running `chewing demo tests/fixtures/sample_chewing_1.mp4 -o /tmp/cv_demo_out/` exits 0.

Output dir contains: frame_signals_ours.csv, frame_signals_orofac.csv, labels_ours.csv, labels_orofac.csv, events_ours.csv, events_orofac.csv, bouts_ours.csv, bouts_orofac.csv, summary.json, signals.png, demo.mp4.
README.md exists with sections: Overview, Installation, Quickstart, CLI Reference, Output Schemas, License + Attribution.
README Quickstart shows the exact command above and the expected console output snippet.
README contains attribution: 'Uses orofacIAnalysis (MIT) by Cameron Maloney — see ATTRIBUTION.md.'
Running `python -c 'import chewing; print(chewing.__version__)'` prints '0.1.0'.
`chewing --help` exits 0 and lists all 6 subcommands.
stories.verificationEvidence: AC1: `.venv/bin/chewing demo tests/fixtures/sample_chewing_1.mp4 -o /tmp/cv_demo_out/` exits 0 and prints summary table plus `demo outputs written to /tmp/cv_demo_out/`. AC2: /tmp/cv_demo_out contains all required outputs: frame_signals_ours.csv, frame_signals_orofac.csv, labels_ours.csv, labels_orofac.csv, events_ours.csv, events_orofac.csv, bouts_ours.csv, bouts_orofac.csv, summary.json, signals.png, demo.mp4; demo.mp4 size=50,629,193 bytes and signals.png size=152,696 bytes. AC3: README.md exists with sections Overview, Installation, Quickstart, CLI Reference, Output Schemas, License + Attribution. AC4: README Quickstart shows exact command `.venv/bin/chewing demo tests/fixtures/sample_chewing_1.mp4 -o /tmp/cv_demo_out/` and expected console output snippet. AC5: README contains exact attribution `Uses orofacIAnalysis (MIT) by Cameron Maloney — see ATTRIBUTION.md.` AC6: `.venv/bin/python -c 'import chewing; print(chewing.__version__)'` prints 0.1.0; `chewing.version` alias also prints 0.1.0. AC7: `.venv/bin/chewing --help` exits 0 and lists analyze, plot, overlay, eval, compare, demo. Full pytest battery `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/` passes 55/55.
